title: tabernaemontana johnstonii
intro: Tabernaemontana johnstonii, also known as the Johnston's tree or Johnston's trumpet vine, is a species of flowering plant in the Apocynaceae family, native to the tropical forests of Papua New Guinea. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Tabernaemontana johnstonii include its ability to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health. The plant is typically prepared as a decoction or infusion, with the leaves and stems being used in herbal preparations, although further research is needed to fully understand its effects and potential interactions.
